response:
Sec. 1  
peanuts are a type of food that contains proteins which can trigger an allergic reaction in some people  
the human body has a system that detects harmful substances and reacts to them  
allergic reactions can cause severe symptoms such as difficulty breathing and swelling of the lips  
these reactions are not related to the physical properties of the substance itself  
the allergic response is a biological process that occurs in the immune system  
underwater environments do not contain peanuts or any other food items  
the question is based on a misunderstanding of how allergies work  
the immune system does not recognize peanuts as a threat in the same way it does other substances  

Sec. 2  
allergies are triggered by the immune system responding to a substance that is not harmful to most people  
the reaction is not related to the physical properties of the substance  
the allergic response is a biological process that occurs in the immune system  
the immune system does not recognize peanuts as a threat in the same way it does other substances  
the question is based on a misunderstanding of how allergies work  
the immune system does not recognize peanuts as a threat in the same way it does other substances
